title: Gap Analysis and Action Plan
verbatim: A financial institution must perform a gap analysis of existing practices in managing technology risk against the requirements in this policy document and highlight key implementation gaps. The financial institution must develop an action plan with a clear timeline and key milestones to address the gaps identified. The gap analysis and action plan must be submitted to the Bank no later than 90 days after the issuance date of this policy document. Financial institutions that have previously made a submission in accordance with the equivalent provision in the previous version of this policy document are required to maintain continuous compliance by identifying any new gaps against the enhanced or revised requirements in the latest version of this policy document and taking the necessary steps to address such gaps. The updated annual assessment of its level of compliance must be made available to the Bank upon request.
translation: Conduct a formal annual self-assessment of your compliance with the full RMiT policy. For each requirement, determine whether you are fully compliant, partially compliant, or non-compliant, and provide supporting evidence for your assessment.
evidence: Compliance status for each RMiT clause
Board or senior management review of assessment results

artifacts.name: Annual RMiT Self-Assessment Report
artifacts.description: Comprehensive self-assessment of compliance with all RMiT requirements

artifacts.name: Compliance Evidence Package
artifacts.description: Supporting evidence for compliance ratings across all clauses

artifacts.name: Board Review of Self-Assessment
artifacts.description: Board or senior management review and sign-off of self-assessment results

title: Submission of Gap Analysis to Relevant Department
verbatim: The self-assessment, gap analysis and action plan in paragraph 18.1 must be submitted to Jabatan Penyeliaan Konglomerat Kewangan, Jabatan Penyeliaan Perbankan, Jabatan Penyeliaan Insurans dan Takaful or Jabatan Pemantauan Perkhidmatan Pembayaran, as the case may be.
translation: For every compliance gap found in the self-assessment, create a remediation plan with specific actions, assigned owners, target dates, and resource requirements. Report remediation progress to the board at least quarterly.
evidence: Gap remediation plan with actions and owners
Target completion dates and resource requirements
Quarterly progress reports to the board

artifacts.name: Gap Remediation Plan
artifacts.description: Detailed remediation plan for all identified RMiT compliance gaps

artifacts.name: Quarterly Remediation Progress Report
artifacts.description: Quarterly report on remediation progress presented to the board

artifacts.name: Remediation Completion Evidence
artifacts.description: Evidence of completed remediation actions for closed gaps
